name: Asian Grill

notes: Terra reconciled all 257 frozen rows against the current restaurant-linked Toast menu; Sol confirmed the restaurant identity and current menu surface.
Post-repair recheck reconciled every frozen item to the repaired generated restaurant.

Toast directly supports positive signals explicitly named in restaurant-authored item names or descriptions, but it is not a comprehensive allergen matrix, complete ingredient list, or cross-contact policy.
Sol rejected Terra's proposed blanket downgrade and limited the discrepancy to claims inferred from culinary names without explicit restaurant disclosure.
87 items retain only positive signals directly supported by restaurant-authored names or descriptions; 170 items are accurately unavailable.
No negative or cross-contact claim is inferred from the absence of a comprehensive guide.
The toast-menu extraction/repair path must preserve directly disclosed ingredients while removing only unsupported culinary-term inferences.
Scoped restaurant repair removes unsupported cake and wonton culinary inference while retaining explicit item-text disclosures.

sourceAttempts.outcome: Official domain redirected but did not provide a usable current allergen or ingredient source.
Restaurant-linked Toast menu was accessible to the research agents and supplied identity, menu, and selected ingredient disclosures.
Live Toast menu reviewed; no comprehensive allergen matrix, allergen legend, complete ingredients, or cross-contact statement was published.
No separate restaurant-issued allergen, nutrition, ingredient, or cross-contact guide was located.
No relevant official archived allergen or ingredient document was located.
Third-party menu corroborated identity and selected menu descriptions but was not used as allergen authority.

findings.summary: A scoped subset of generated allergen claims was inferred from culinary names without explicit support in restaurant-authored Toast text.
findings.resolution: Removed unsupported signals from six cake/wonton items and narrowed Crab Cake to explicit shellfish; retained direct ingredient disclosures elsewhere.

findings.summary: The current restaurant-linked menu contains selected positive ingredient disclosures but no comprehensive allergen matrix, complete ingredients, allergen legend, negative-claim coverage, or cross-contact policy.
findings.resolution: The limitation is accurately represented: only direct positive disclosures are retained, remaining items are unavailable, and no negative or cross-contact conclusions are published.

repairs.summary: Removed unsupported cake and wonton culinary-name allergen inferences while preserving allergens directly disclosed by restaurant-authored item names and descriptions; Crab Cake retains only explicit shellfish.
verificationCommands: node --test --test-name-pattern='Asian Grill preserves direct menu disclosures' scripts/restaurant-pipeline.test.mjs
